articles.title: Personal decision for Starmer on whether to fight leadership contest, minister says
articles.description: Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy tells the BBC that the prime minister has "shown before that he's up for a fight" and she "wouldn't write [him] off".

articles.title: The UK is churning through leaders. Is the country becoming harder to govern?
articles.description: Discontent with the political class is greater than ever - has the UK become ungovernable?

articles.title: Forty-three arrests after £4.5m police operation to keep rival London protests apart
articles.description: Serious clashes between Unite the Kingdom and pro-Palestinian protesters were avoided on Saturday.

articles.title: 'Look Mum, one point': Why does the UK keep getting Eurovision wrong?
articles.description: After four years of flops, the BBC has to think hard about how it approaches Eurovision in 2027.
